treatment_information.specific_treatment: total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
treatment_information.treatment_response: prolonged recovery period of several years

treatment_information.specific_treatment: vitamins A, C and E supplements

treatment_timeline.event: total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
excision of basal cell carcinoma on right upper lip
excision of 10 cm pedunculated polyp from sigmoid colon with focus of intramucosal carcinoma

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: annual breast examinations and MRI, annual skin examinations, annual colonoscopy and upper gastrointestinal endoscopy
patient_outcome.complications: diabetic complications leading to loss of sight in right eye, cirrhosis of the liver, osteoporosis, intermittent viral labyrinthitis, fungal toenail infections, episodes of urticaria
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: shortened life expectancy, no patient with Bloom syndrome has yet been reported to have survived into their fifties
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: prolonged recovery period of several years after hysterectomy
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: reluctant to undergo further major surgery due to prolonged recovery period after hysterectomy
